templates.template: Azure Microsoft SQL DTU Database by HTTP
templates.name: Azure Microsoft SQL DTU Database by HTTP
templates.description:
This template is designed to monitor Microsoft SQL DTU-based databases via HTTP.
It works without any external scripts and uses the script item.

Setup:
  1. Create an Azure service principal via the Azure command-line interface (Azure CLI) for your subscription.
    `az ad sp create-for-rbac --name zabbix --role reader --scope /subscriptions/<subscription_id>`
    See https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/create-an-azure-service-principal-azure-cli for more details.
  2. Link the template to a host.
  3. Configure the macros: {$AZURE.APP.ID}, {$AZURE.PASSWORD}, {$AZURE.TENANT.ID}, {$AZURE.SUBSCRIPTION.ID} and {$AZURE.RESOURCE.ID}.

You can discuss this template or leave feedback on our forum https://www.zabbix.com/forum/zabbix-suggestions-and-feedback/

Generated by official Zabbix template tool "Templator"
templates.readme:
## Overview

This template is designed to monitor Microsoft SQL DTU-based databases via HTTP.
It works without any external scripts and uses the script item.

## Setup

1. Create an Azure service principal via the Azure command-line interface (Azure CLI) for your subscription.

      `az ad sp create-for-rbac --name zabbix --role reader --scope /subscriptions/<subscription_id>`

> See [Azure documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/create-an-azure-service-principal-azure-cli) for more details.

2. Link the template to a host.
3. Configure the host wizard configuration fields: `Azure App ID`, `Azure password`, `Azure tenant ID`, `Azure subscription ID` and `Azure SQL DTU-based database ID`.

items.name: Availability status detailed
items.description: A detailed summary of the availability status.

items.name: Availability state
items.description: The availability status of the resource.

triggers.expression: last(/Azure Microsoft SQL DTU Database by HTTP/azure.db.mssql.availability.state)=1
triggers.name: Azure MSSQL DTU: Microsoft SQL database is degraded
triggers.description: The resource is in a degraded state.

triggers.expression: last(/Azure Microsoft SQL DTU Database by HTTP/azure.db.mssql.availability.state)=3
triggers.name: Azure MSSQL DTU: Microsoft SQL database is in unknown state

triggers.expression: last(/Azure Microsoft SQL DTU Database by HTTP/azure.db.mssql.availability.state)=2
triggers.name: Azure MSSQL DTU: Microsoft SQL database is unavailable

items.name: Connections failed: System errors
items.description: The number of failed connections with system errors.

items.name: Connections successful
items.description: The number of successful connections.

items.name: CPU percentage
items.description: The average percentage of CPU usage on a host.

triggers.expression: min(/Azure Microsoft SQL DTU Database by HTTP/azure.db.mssql.cpu.percentage,5m)>{$AZURE.DB.CPU.UTIL.CRIT}
triggers.name: Azure MSSQL DTU: High CPU utilization
triggers.description: The CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

items.name: Get errors
items.description: A list of errors from API requests.

triggers.expression: length(last(/Azure Microsoft SQL DTU Database by HTTP/azure.db.mssql.data.errors))>0
triggers.name: Azure MSSQL DTU: There are errors in requests to API
triggers.description: Zabbix has received errors in response to API requests.

items.name: Get data
items.description: The result of API requests is expressed in JSON format.

items.name: Data IO percentage
items.description: The average percentage of physical data read.

items.name: Deadlocks
items.description: The number of deadlocks. Not applicable to data warehouses.

items.name: DTU limit
items.description: The DTU limit. Applicable to DTU-based databases.

items.name: DTU percentage
items.description: The average percentage of DTU consumption for a DTU-based database.

triggers.expression: min(/Azure Microsoft SQL DTU Database by HTTP/azure.db.mssql.dtu.percentage,5m)>{$AZURE.DB.DTU.UTIL.CRIT}
triggers.name: Azure MSSQL DTU: High DTU utilization
triggers.description: The DTU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

items.name: DTU used
items.description: The DTU used. Applicable to DTU-based databases.

items.name: Connections blocked by firewall
items.description: The number of connections blocked by the firewall.

items.name: Log IO percentage
items.description: The percentage of I/O used for log writes. Not applicable to data warehouses.

items.name: SQL instance CPU percent
items.description: CPU usage from all user and system workloads. Not applicable to data warehouses.

items.name: SQL instance memory percent
items.description: The percentage of memory used by the database engine instance. Not applicable to data warehouses.

items.name: Sessions count
items.description: The number of active sessions. Not applicable to Synapse DW Analytics.

items.name: Sessions percentage
items.description: The percentage of sessions. Not applicable to data warehouses.

items.name: Data space allocated
items.description: The allocated data storage. Not applicable to data warehouses.

items.name: Log backup storage size
items.description: The cumulative log backup storage size. Applies to vCore-based and Hyperscale databases.

items.name: Data space used percent
items.description: Data space used in percent. Not applicable to data warehouses or Hyperscale databases.

triggers.expression: last(/Azure Microsoft SQL DTU Database by HTTP/azure.db.mssql.storage.percent)>{$AZURE.DB.STORAGE.PUSED.CRIT}
triggers.name: Azure MSSQL DTU: Storage space is critically low
triggers.description: Critical utilization of the storage space.

triggers.expression: last(/Azure Microsoft SQL DTU Database by HTTP/azure.db.mssql.storage.percent)>{$AZURE.DB.STORAGE.PUSED.WARN}
triggers.name: Azure MSSQL DTU: Storage space is low
triggers.description: High utilization of the storage space.

dependencies.name: Azure MSSQL DTU: Storage space is critically low
dependencies.expression: last(/Azure Microsoft SQL DTU Database by HTTP/azure.db.mssql.storage.percent)>{$AZURE.DB.STORAGE.PUSED.CRIT}

items.name: Data space used
items.description: Data space used. Not applicable to data warehouses.

items.name: In-Memory OLTP storage percent
items.description: In-Memory OLTP storage percent. Not applicable to data warehouses.

items.name: Tempdb data file size
items.description: The space used in `tempdb` data files, expressed in bytes. Not applicable to data warehouses.

items.name: Tempdb log used percent
items.description: The percentage of space used in the `tempdb` transaction log file. Not applicable to data warehouses.

items.name: Tempdb log file size
items.description: The space used in the `tempdb` transaction log file, expressed in bytes. Not applicable to data warehouses.

items.name: Workers percentage
items.description: The percentage of workers. Not applicable to data warehouses.

macros.description: The App ID of Microsoft Azure.
The critical threshold of CPU utilization, expressed in %.

macros.description: The critical threshold of memory utilization, expressed in %.

macros.description: The critical threshold of storage utilization, expressed in %.

config.label: Storage utilization critical threshold
config.description: Critical threshold of storage utilization, expressed in %. In the range from 0 to 100 inclusive.

macros.description: The warning threshold of storage utilization, expressed in %.

config.label: Storage utilization warning threshold
config.description: Warning threshold of storage utilization, expressed in %. In the range from 0 to 100 inclusive.

macros.description: Sets the HTTP proxy value. If this macro is empty, then no proxy is used.

config.label: HTTP proxy
config.description: HTTP proxy to use for requests.

macros.description: Microsoft Azure SQL DTU-based database ID.
